text_blocks.text: has lost most of its tradition for musical
improvisation, from the Baroque era to the Romantic
era, there are examples of performers who could
improvise in the style of their era. In the Baroque era,
organ performers would improve preludes, keyboard
performers playing harpsichord would improvise chords
from the figured bass symbols beneath the bass notes
of the basso continuo part and both vocal and
ornaments. J.S. Bach was particularly noted for his
complex improvisations. During the Classical era, the
composer-performer Mozart was noted for his ability to
improvise melodies in different styles. During the
Classical era, some virtuoso soloists would improvise the
cadenza sections of a concerto. During the Romantic era,
Beethoven would improvise at the piano. For more
